name: DYSPLASIA EPIPHYSEALIS HEMIMELICA
definition: TREVOR DISEASE = TARSOEPIPHYSEAL ACLASIS uncommon skeletal developmental disorder representing an epiphyseal osteochondroma

demographics.Path: lobulated mass protruding from epiphysis with a cartilaginous cap
demographics.Histo: normal bone + hyaline cartilage with abundant enchondral ossification (= abnormal

dominant_finding: pelvic involvement: femoral head, symphysis pubis, triradiate cartilage
distribution: disorganized epiphyseal calcification accompanied by irregular ossification
discriminating_features: pelvic involvement: femoral head, symphysis pubis, triradiate cartilage
premature appearance of an eccentric, lobulated, overgrown, asymmetric ossification
disorganized epiphyseal calcification accompanied by irregular ossification

general: pelvic involvement: femoral head, symphysis pubis, triradiate cartilage
premature appearance of an eccentric, lobulated, overgrown, asymmetric ossification
Childhood: disorganized epiphyseal calcification accompanied by irregular ossification
osteochondroma-like growth from one side of epiphysis
premature closure of physis results in limb deformity and limb length discrepancy
irregular articular surface combined with angular deformity
undertubulation of bone as a consequence of secondary involvement of metaphysis
